issues.claim: Your jurisdiction extends only to the action itself — the function you can refactor, the migration you can execute, the deploy you can run through the pipeline.
issues.objection: 'Jurisdiction' is a competent rendering of adhikāra but loses the dual sense of right + qualification (eligibility-to-perform). Shankara's bhāṣya explicitly contrasts adhikāra-in-karman with adhikāra-in-jñāna-niṣṭhā; the engineering layer does not preserve this contrast and does not need to, but a reader will not learn from this layer that adhikāra carries the eligibility / who-is-qualified connotation. Acceptable simplification, not distortion.

notes: v3.1 verification. The engineering layer now explicitly preserves all three distinct prohibitions of the second hemistich: (i) karmaṇy eva adhikāras te / mā phaleṣu kadācana — 'First: your jurisdiction extends only to the action itself ... The fruits ... are never your jurisdiction, at any time.' (ii) mā karma-phala-hetuḥ bhūḥ — 'Second: do not regard yourself as the cause of those fruits ... To inwardly claim authorship of the result is to bind yourself as the agent of consequences you cannot control.' This faithfully renders Shankara's gloss that desire for fruits binds the actor as the agent of those results (kartā tasya phalasya). (iii) mā te saṅgaḥ astu akarmaṇi — 'Third: do not let this become a license for retreat ... inaction disguised as caution, and it is rejected here just as firmly as the grasping for fruits.' This faithfully renders both Shankara's note on aversion-to-action being rejected and Ramanuja's emphasis that Arjuna's proposed inaction is rejected. Compared to the prior pass, the prior minor flag on 'make the work itself the motive' (which brushed against a Mimamsa-style auto-telic reading) has been removed — that phrasing is gone from v3.1. The remaining 'jurisdiction' nit on adhikāra-as-eligibility is a single minor point. One minor < three; PASS. Cross-persona second-hemistich-compression signal: RESOLVED.
